message:
feat(engine): stateful comment and string literal parsing in ambient authority source auditor

The source auditor previously used a naive inline-comment stripper that
failed to handle block comments (/* ... */), nested block comments,
string literals, and raw/byte strings. Patterns appearing inside any of
these constructs were incorrectly flagged as ambient authority violations.

Changes:
- Replace the stateless inline_comment_start() function with a new
  sanitized_code_portion() that tracks LineScanState across lines:
  in_string, raw_hashes (for r#"..."# literals), block_comment_depth
- Block comments (including nested) are fully suppressed — any pattern
  match inside /* ... */ is ignored even across multiple lines
- String literals (regular, raw r#"..."#, and byte b"...") are
  suppressed — patterns inside quoted strings no longer trigger findings
- Real code following a block comment or string literal on the same
  line is still scanned correctly

Tests added:
- Patterns inside single-line block comments → ignored
- Patterns inside multi-line block comments → ignored
- Patterns inside nested block comments → ignored
- Patterns inside string literals → ignored
- Patterns inside raw/byte strings → ignored
- Real pattern after block comment on same line → detected
- Real pattern beside documentation string → detected

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
